signals.reasoning: BTC is constructive, but the active veto keeps benchmark exposure in watch mode rather than action mode.
ETH has mild bullish tone, but not enough edge to justify fresh risk under the event brake.
SOL is constructive but too average to justify action while veto is active.
ARB is the strongest long on raw mechanics because the squeeze profile is still intact, but the event brake should keep it watch-only rather than buyable here.
REZ has a decent squeeze-long profile, but not enough quality to overcome the veto state.
WLFI is still weak overall, but the edge is not clean enough to justify a fresh short right here.
FOGO remains a valid bearish continuation because the tape is still weak and funding is supportive for the short side.
DYDX lacks enough clean edge to justify action here.
DASH remains a decent watch-only long, but veto keeps it out of the buy bucket.
STBL remains the cleanest bearish fade because funding is strongly positive, crowding is high, and the market still looks vulnerable to reversal.
